SG.visa: No visa required (30-day free entry for Indian passport holders)
SG.health: No mandatory vaccinations. Yellow fever certificate if arriving from endemic country.
SG.money: Singapore Dollar (SGD). Cards widely accepted. ATMs everywhere.

GB.visa: UK visa required. Apply via VFS Global. Processing: 3-6 weeks.
GB.money: British Pound (GBP). Contactless widely accepted. Oyster card for transport.

JP.visa: No visa required for tourism (90-day free entry). Must have return ticket.
JP.money: Japanese Yen (JPY). Cash is king — many small shops don't accept cards. ATMs at 7-Eleven and post offices.

US.visa: B1/B2 visa or ESTA required. Interview may be needed at US embassy.
US.money: US Dollar (USD). Cards accepted almost everywhere. Tipping 18-20% expected.

SG.visa: No visa required (90-day free entry for US passport holders)

GB.visa: No visa required (6-month visitor, electronic travel authorisation may apply)

IN.visa: India eVisa required (30-day tourist). Apply online, processing 72 hours.
IN.health: No mandatory vaccinations but recommended: Hepatitis A/B, Typhoid.
IN.money: Indian Rupee (INR). Cards accepted at most hotels/restaurants. Cash useful at markets.

default.visa: Check visa requirements at your embassy website.
default.health: No mandatory vaccinations. Check WHO recommendations.
default.money: Check local currency. Carry backup payment method.
default.emergency: Emergency number varies by country. Common: 112 (international)
